exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter.
instructions: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter. Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight lumbar arch.
Grip: Use a close grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower — roughly 6–12 inches apart) with a pronated grip, wrists stacked over wrists and the bar.
Scapula and position: Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable chest platform; keep shoulders down away from ears.
Unrack: Drive your feet, take a deep breath, brace your core, lock your elbows to lift the bar out of the rack and position it over your sternum with arms vertical.
Descent: Lower the bar in a controlled manner to the lower chest/sternum while keeping your elbows tucked close to your torso (avoid wide flaring), inhaling as you descend.
Touch and pause: Lightly touch or stop just above the chest (avoid bouncing) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Press: Exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows, keeping the bar path slightly diagonal toward the head until near full extension (do not hyperextend or lock aggressively).
Re-rack: After the set, control the bar back to the rack hooks or have a spotter assist. Maintain form on every rep.

Coaching cues & safety: Use a spotter or safety pins for heavy sets, keep wrists neutral, avoid flaring elbows to protect shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow tuck and controlled descent.
exercises.description: A supine barbell press using a narrow grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the lower chest/sternum with elbows kept close to the torso and then pressed back to lockout.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft, and the barbell held in front of your thighs with a shoulder-width underhand (palms up) grip.
Keep your torso tall, chest up, and elbows pinned to your sides; this is the starting position with arms nearly fully extended (no locked hyperextension).
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the forearms toward the shoulders while keeping the upper arms stationary.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top when the bar reaches shoulder level; avoid shrugging or leaning back.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with a controlled motion until the arms are nearly extended.
Maintain strict form throughout—do not swing the torso or use excessive momentum; engage the core to stabilize and keep the delts relaxed.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with an underhand (supinated) grip to isolate the biceps by flexing the elbow and lifting the bar toward the shoulders, then lowering with control.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Standing French Press
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft, holding an EZ bar overhead with a neutral (angled) grip and hands close to shoulder width.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees soft, holding an EZ bar overhead with a neutral (angled) grip and hands close to shoulder width.
Brace your core and keep your chest up and spine neutral; avoid overarching the lower back throughout the movement.
Raise the bar overhead so your arms are straight, then slowly lower the bar behind your head by flexing only at the elbows until your forearms are near your triceps or just below ear level.
Keep your upper arms stationary and pointing up (elbows tucked in toward the head); do not let the elbows drift out or flare wide.
Pause briefly at the bottom with a controlled stretch, then press the bar back up by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight (avoid locking out aggressively).
Breathe in as you lower the bar and exhale as you press it up. Maintain a firm grip and neutral wrist position to avoid strain.
Perform repetitions with controlled tempo and stop if you feel excessive elbow or shoulder pain; reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow position or a neutral spine.
exercises.description: Standing overhead French press using an EZ bar to isolate the triceps by flexing and extending the elbows with the bar lowered behind the head. The exercise is performed upright with the upper arms kept vertical and the core braced to prevent lumbar arching.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown (V bar)
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a V-bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Setup: Attach a V-bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight. Stand facing the machine, feet about hip-width apart.
Grip and posture: Grasp the V-bar with a neutral (palms facing) grip. Step slightly back so the cable is taut, hinge very slightly at the hips, chest up, shoulders back, and eyes forward.
Starting position: Tuck your elbows close to your sides and bend the elbows so the V-bar is near chest/upper abdomen level. Keep your upper arms stationary and vertical throughout the movement.

Downward movement (concentric): Exhale and press the bar down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and the triceps are contracted. Keep the wrists neutral and avoid letting the elbows drift forward.
End position: Pause briefly at full extension without locking the elbows aggressively, maintaining tension on the triceps.

Return (eccentric): Inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position by bending the elbows, controlling the weight to avoid momentum. Stop when the forearms reach roughly parallel to the floor or the bar returns to chest/upper abdomen level.

Technique tips: Keep the torso stable and avoid using body swing or stepping to generate force. If you feel the delts or upper back taking over, reduce weight and re-establish tight elbows at your sides.
Repetitions and safety: Perform 8–15 controlled reps per set depending on goal; choose a weight that allows strict form. Stop if you feel sharp pain in the elbow or wrist.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using a V-bar to extend the elbows and isolate the triceps; performed from a standing position with the upper arms held stationary at the sides. Emphasize a full elbow extension and controlled return to work the long and lateral heads of the triceps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft and core engaged; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides in a supinated (palms facing forward) grip.
Keep your torso upright and shoulders back; allow the elbows to rest close to your ribs and remain stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by flexing at the elbow, moving only the forearms so the dumbbells travel toward the front of the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement when the dumbbells reach shoulder level; do not lean back or use momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled extension of the elbows until the arms are straight.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, maintaining a neutral wrist and avoiding swinging the body; if you must swing, reduce the weight.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the elbow flexors by lifting weights from the thighs to shoulder level using a supinated grip. It builds biceps strength and size while the forearms assist in gripping and stabilizing the load.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the EZ bar with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width on the angled sections of the bar; wrists should be neutral and aligned with forearms.
instructions: Sit on a preacher (angled) curl bench with feet flat on the floor and chest/abdomen lightly against the pad so your upper arms rest flat on the sloped pad; body should be stable and upright.
Reach forward and grasp the EZ bar with a supinated (underhand) grip about shoulder-width on the angled sections of the bar; wrists should be neutral and aligned with forearms.
Start with your arms extended and the bar held just above the pad; this is the start position (elbows slightly bent but not locked).

Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing at the elbows, keeping the upper arms pressed against the pad and avoiding shoulder movement; focus on contracting the biceps as the bar approaches chest level.
Pause and squeeze the biceps briefly at the top of the movement without lifting the elbows off the pad or swinging the torso.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to the start position until the arms are near full extension, maintaining contact between upper arms and pad throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, using a weight that allows strict form; avoid using momentum or arching the back to lift the bar.
If wrists or forearms fatigue prematurely, reduce weight or shorten range slightly to maintain strict elbow-driven motion and protect joints.
exercises.description: Seated preacher curl using an EZ bar to isolate the biceps by stabilizing the upper arms on an angled bench pad; emphasizes controlled elbow flexion with minimal shoulder involvement. The angled pad reduces cheating and increases time under tension for the biceps.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.description: A supine barbell press using a narrow grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the lower chest/sternum with elbows kept close to the torso and then pressed back to lockout.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Grip the EZ bar on the angled (inner) hand positions so your palms are facing upward/neutral; hands about shoulder-width apart.
Keep your chest up, shoulders back, and core braced to stabilize the spine; elbows should sit close to your sides.
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing your elbows, driving the movement with your biceps; keep your upper arms stationary and forearms moving.
Pause briefly at the top when the bar is near shoulder level and biceps are fully contracted; avoid shrugging or swinging the torso.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to full elbow extension without locking the joints.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and strict form throughout.
exercises.description: Standing curl using an EZ bar with a semi-supinated grip to isolate elbow flexion and develop the biceps. Perform controlled concentric and eccentric reps while keeping the torso still to minimize cheating.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Spider Curl
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place it so you can lie face-down (prone) with your chest on the pad and feet braced on the floor.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and place it so you can lie face-down (prone) with your chest on the pad and feet braced on the floor.
Load an EZ curl bar with an appropriate weight and grasp the bar with a supinated (underhand) grip using the angled grips so your hands are roughly shoulder-width apart.
Lie prone on the bench with your chest and abdomen supported, let your arms hang straight down perpendicular to the floor, and keep your neck neutral — this is the start position.

Keep your elbows fixed in place (close to vertical) and exhale as you curl the bar upward by contracting the biceps until the forearms approach the biceps or you reach a full, comfortable contraction.
Pause briefly at the top to emphasize peak contraction, keeping the upper arms stationary and avoiding shoulder movement or torso swing.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the fully extended start position under control, resisting momentum so the biceps remain loaded.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Maintain strict form throughout: chest supported, elbows stable, controlled tempo, and avoid using the lower back or shoulders to lift.
exercises.description: A prone (spider) curl performed on an incline bench using an EZ bar; chest is supported on the bench while the upper arms hang vertical so the biceps do isolated concentric work with minimal torso involvement. Focus on strict elbow flexion and a slow controlled descent to maximize biceps tension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing French Press
exercises.notes: Stand tall in a neutral, upright position with feet about shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft (not locked).
instructions: Stand tall in a neutral, upright position with feet about shoulder-width apart and knees slightly soft (not locked).
Grip a single dumbbell with both hands (palms supporting one end) and lift it overhead until your arms are extended.
Keep your upper arms (elbows) pointing up and close to your head throughout the movement; brace your abs and maintain a neutral spine.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending only at the elbows until your forearms are roughly parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the triceps.
Pause briefly at the bottom while keeping the elbows stationary and upper arms vertical.
Exhale and press the weight back up by extending the elbows until the arms are nearly straight; avoid locking the elbows at the top.
Perform controlled repetitions, keeping the shoulders (delts) and forearms stable and the core engaged to prevent leaning or arching.
If using heavier weight causes the elbows to flare or the torso to rock, reduce the load or perform the exercise seated for additional stability.
exercises.description: A standing overhead triceps extension performed with a single dumbbell held behind the head to isolate and strengthen the triceps while the shoulders and core stabilize the movement.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley and attach a rope (or straight bar).
instructions: Set a high pulley and attach a rope (or straight bar). Select an appropriate weight that allows controlled movement.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and a slight forward lean from the hips; brace your core.
Grasp the rope with a neutral (palms facing) grip and bring your elbows to your sides so your upper arms are vertical and stationary.
Exhale and press the rope down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and the rope ends are slightly spread apart at the bottom.
Pause briefly at full extension while keeping shoulders down and upper arms fixed; avoid using torso swing or stepping for momentum.
Inhale and slowly allow the cable to raise your hands back to the start position, controlling the eccentric phase until your forearms return to vertical.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining tucked elbows and a stable torso throughout the set.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdowns (rope attachment) target the triceps by extending the elbow against resistance while the upper arms remain stationary. The movement also engages the forearms for grip and the delts and abs for posture and stabilization.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing forward (supinated grip) and arms fully extended.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and fixed in position throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by bending your elbows until the weights reach shoulder level, squeezing the biceps at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining upright posture and avoid leaning back or using momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with full elbow extension under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on strict form and minimizing shoulder or torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the biceps by flexing the elbow against resistance while standing upright; perform controlled reps without swinging to maximize tension on the biceps.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Lying Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and back pressed into the bench.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and back pressed into the bench.
Grip an EZ bar with a narrow to shoulder-width grip so wrists are comfortable and slightly angled; hold the bar directly over your chest with arms fully extended.
Brace your core and keep your upper arms vertical and stationary (elbows pointing toward the ceiling) throughout the movement.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar by bending only at the elbows, bringing the bar toward your forehead or slightly behind the head (depending on comfort) while maintaining the upper-arm position.
Pause briefly when the forearms approach the target point (forehead/behind head) without letting the elbows flare excessively forward.
Exhale and extend the elbows to press the bar back up to the starting position, fully contracting the triceps but avoiding locking the elbows aggressively.
Maintain controlled tempo, keep wrists neutral on the EZ bar, avoid excessive shoulder movement or arching the lower back, and repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Supine (lying) skull-crusher variation using an EZ curl bar to isolate the triceps by extending the elbows from a flexed position toward full extension. Performed on a flat bench with the upper arms kept mostly stationary to maximize triceps tension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hammer Curl
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip and wrists aligned with forearms.
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip and wrists aligned with forearms.
Keep your elbows tucked to your sides and stationary throughout the movement; avoid letting them drift forward or backward.

Exhale and curl both dumbbells upward by flexing at the elbow, keeping palms facing each other and maintaining a straight wrist; lift until the dumbbells reach near shoulder level and you feel a contraction in the biceps.
Pause and squeeze at the top for 1 second while keeping the forearms and wrists neutral.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled tempo until arms are fully extended but not locked out.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding body swing or using momentum; if momentum appears, reduce weight or perform the exercise seated or with back support.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell hammer curl performed with a neutral (thumbs-up) grip to emphasize the biceps while also recruiting the forearm muscles; performed slowly with controlled motion and minimal torso movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip).
instructions: Start standing upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip).
Brace your core, keep a neutral spine and chest lifted, and look forward throughout the movement.
Begin the descent by sending your hips back as if sitting into a chair, bending at the hips and knees simultaneously; keep weight distributed through your heels and mid-foot.
Lower until your thighs are roughly parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows) while ensuring your knees track over your toes and do not cave inward.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in your abs and legs.
Drive through your heels and mid-foot to extend the hips and knees, returning to standing while exhaling and keeping the dumbbells at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining controlled tempo; reduce range or weight if you cannot hold a neutral spine or if knees collapse inward.
exercises.description: A dumbbell squat is a standing, loaded lower-body squat performed while holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides; it primarily works the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes and posterior chain. It trains squat mechanics, lower-body strength, and core stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Single Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (or one dumbbell in each hand if preferred), feet hip-width apart.
instructions: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (or one dumbbell in each hand if preferred), feet hip-width apart.

Shift your weight onto one foot and lift the opposite foot slightly off the floor so you are balancing on a single leg (standing position, single-leg). Keep the supporting knee straight but not locked and torso tall.
Brace your core and maintain a neutral spine; if needed lightly touch a wall or hold a sturdy object for balance (wall-assisted) without leaning.

Slowly raise your heel by plantarflexing the ankle of the supporting leg until you are on the ball of your foot/toes, squeezing the calf at the top. Keep shoulders relaxed and dumbbells at your sides.
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top while maintaining balance and control.
Lower your heel slowly back to the starting position until the sole is flat on the floor (or slightly below the step edge if using a raised surface for increased range of motion).
Complete the desired number of repetitions on that leg, then switch to the other leg and repeat.
exercises.description: A single-leg standing calf raise with dumbbells isolates the calf (plantarflexors) on one side while challenging balance and stability. It builds strength and size in the calves while the core, grip, and leg stabilizers assist.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.
Sit upright with feet under the roller pad, grip the side handles or seat edges for stability, and keep your chest lifted and core engaged.
Start with knees bent to about 90 degrees (or the machine's starting position) and inhale to prepare.
Exhale and extend your legs by straightening the knees until they are almost fully extended — do not lock the knees at the top.
Pause briefly (0.5–1 second) at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position with a controlled eccentric motion until knees return to the initial bend.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding momentum or bouncing.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knees against a padded lever. The exercise focuses on controlled knee extension and eccentric lowering to target the front of the thighs.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back flat against the backrest, feet under the roller pad and hands holding the side handles or seat edges for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or slightly less) and toes pointing forward; brace your core to keep the torso still.
Exhale and extend your knees by contracting the quads, pushing the roller pad upward until your legs are nearly straight — do not lock the knees.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight with control back to the starting knee angle, keeping tension in the quads throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Avoid using momentum, excessive trunk lean, or hyperextending the knees.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever/machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against a padded roller. The movement is performed from a seated, back-supported position and focuses on controlled knee extension and return.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with knees bent at ~90 degrees and feet hip-width apart; place the balls of your feet on the edge of a weight plate or a raised platform so your heels can drop below the toes.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with knees bent at ~90 degrees and feet hip-width apart; place the balls of your feet on the edge of a weight plate or a raised platform so your heels can drop below the toes.
Hold a dumbbell vertically on each thigh just above the knees, gripping the handles so the weight stays secure against your legs (this engages the forearms to hold the dumbbells).
Keep your torso upright, core engaged, and knees fixed in place (do not straighten the knees) throughout the movement.
Exhale and press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible into a full plantarflexion, squeezing the calves at the top; hold for 1 second.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels back down until you feel a stretch in the calves (allow the heels to drop below the platform), controlling the descent—do not bounce.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and full range of motion. Increase dumbbell weight gradually while preserving form.
exercises.description: Seated calf raises performed with dumbbells resting on the thighs to load the calves while seated; raise the heels by plantarflexing the ankles and lower slowly to stretch the calves. This variation emphasizes the soleus and provides extra load using handheld dumbbells.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Set an appropriate weight on the lever machine and position the ankle/calf cuff or pad behind the working lower leg (just above the heel/ankle).
instructions: Set an appropriate weight on the lever machine and position the ankle/calf cuff or pad behind the working lower leg (just above the heel/ankle).
Stand facing the machine support and lean forward, placing your forearms or chest against the pad/handles so your torso is stable and supported (standing position, body braced).
Place your non-working foot firmly on the floor for support and keep the support-side knee slightly bent to stabilize; the working knee can remain slightly bent as it moves.
Begin with the working thigh roughly in line with your torso (neutral hip). Maintain a neutral spine and avoid arching or rotating the pelvis throughout the movement.
Drive the working leg backward by extending the hip, squeezing the glutes at the top of the movement until the leg is behind the body (do not force hyperextension).
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining pelvic stability, then slowly control the lever back to the start position until the hip returns to neutral.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, then switch legs. Keep movement slow and controlled and do not use momentum from the torso or swinging of the leg.
exercises.description: A machine-based single-leg hip extension performed standing with the torso braced on a support; the working leg pushes a lever backward to extend the hip and drive the leg behind the body, emphasizing glute contraction.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart with the barbell over the mid-foot and toes pointing slightly out.
Approach the bar by hinging at the hips and bend the knees until your shins touch the bar; keep hips higher than a squat but lower than full upright.
Grip the bar just outside your knees with a double overhand or mixed grip; set shoulders slightly in front of the bar and keep the bar close to your shins.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, keep a neutral (flat) spine and chest lifted — avoid rounding the back.
Drive through the heels and simultaneously extend hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body and the torso angle changing minimally at first.
Stand tall at the top by finishing hip extension and squeezing the glutes; do not hyperextend the lower back — shoulders should be back but not excessively retracted.
To descend, hinge at the hips first by pushing them back, then bend the knees once the bar passes them, lowering the bar under control to the floor while maintaining a neutral spine.
Reset your breath and core brace at the bottom for the next repetition; maintain consistent foot placement, bar path, and posture throughout the set.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge strength exercise that lifts a loaded barbell from the floor by extending the hips and knees; it targets the posterior chain with heavy involvement of the hamstrings and glutes while the back and core stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
instructions: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
Unlatch or release the safety so the weight rests on the shoulders and stand tall with a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (not locked).
Inhale, then exhale as you press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, squeezing the calves at the top.
Hold the top position briefly (1 second) while maintaining an upright torso and stable hips.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves.
Control the descent—avoid bouncing—and repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack or secure the lever when finished.
exercises.description: A bilateral standing calf raise performed on a lever/shoulder machine that isolates the calf muscles through plantarflexion. The exercise raises the heels off a platform, then lowers them below platform level for a full stretch.

exercises.name: Landmine Squat with Linebacker Attachment
exercises.notes: Set up the landmine: anchor one end of the barbell and attach the linebacker handle to the loaded end.
instructions: Set up the landmine: anchor one end of the barbell and attach the linebacker handle to the loaded end. Load appropriate weight and stand facing the bar at about chest height.

Stand tall, feet about shoulder-width apart or slightly narrower, toes pointing slightly outward; grasp the linebacker handles and position the attachment across your upper chest/front shoulders (upright, not hunched).
Brace your core (engage abs), pull shoulder blades slightly down and back, and keep chest up with a neutral spine throughout the movement.

Begin the descent by sending your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, lowering until your thighs are roughly parallel to the floor or to a depth that maintains a neutral spine and knee tracking.
Keep knees tracking over toes (avoid letting them cave inward), distribute weight through the mid-foot and heel, and maintain tension in the abs to prevent forward collapse.
Drive back up by pressing through the heels and extending the knees and hips simultaneously to return to standing; fully extend but avoid locking the knees aggressively.
Control each repetition on the way down and up; breathe in on the descent and exhale as you stand. Modify foot width or depth to adjust quad vs glute emphasis.
Stop the set if you feel excessive forward lean, rounding of the lower back, or knee valgus; reduce load or adjust foot position as needed for safe form.
exercises.description: A standing squat variation using a landmine with a linebacker attachment that rests the bar across the upper chest/shoulders to load a front-squat style movement. Emphasizes knee extension to work the quadriceps while the glutes and core stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat and back so your knee lines up with the machine's pivot point and the lower leg pad rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat and back so your knee lines up with the machine's pivot point and the lower leg pad rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
Sit upright with your back flat against the pad, chest up, and grip the side handles for stability; keep the non-working leg relaxed on the floor or slightly bent.
Start with the working knee bent to about 90 degrees (or slightly less) and the foot dorsiflexed (toes up) so the pad contacts above the ankle.
Exhale and extend the working knee by lifting the lower leg until it is almost straight; avoid fully locking the knee at the top.
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize quad contraction while maintaining a controlled posture (abs engaged, back against pad).
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with control, resisting the weight rather than letting it drop.
Repeat for the prescribed repetitions, then switch sides and perform the same steps for the other leg.
exercises.description: Seated single-leg extension on a lever machine that isolates and strengthens the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance. Performed one leg at a time to focus on each quad and correct imbalances.

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.

Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled tension, keeping the movement smooth.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then switch to the other leg; maintain an upright posture and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: A single-leg seated lever extension that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance on a leg extension machine. Performed one leg at a time to improve quad strength and muscular balance.
